name: Pasta Rolls
recipeIngredient: Tomato sauce (spaghetti sauce) in a pinch. Should be smooth.
recipeInstructions:
Make the cheese filling: Mix all cheeses together, then add beaten eggs or egg whites. Grate harder cheeses first for better mixing. This mixture is bland but will improve with tomato sauce and toppings.
Make the pasta: Use your favorite recipe. Green pasta looks neat and tastes great too. Cut pasta into about 6-inch wide strips. For dinner rolls, make them 18 inches long; for appetizers or side dishes, keep them 6-10 inches long.
Assemble the rolls: Lay a piece of pasta flat, spread filling evenly leaving about 1 inch free at the edges (both ends). Roll up tightly but avoid tucking the filling under the roll. Repeat until you have one or two rolls per person. Set aside to finish rolling all pieces.
Wrap in cheesecloth: Cut cheesecloth long enough to wrap a roll with overlap. Wrap each roll, tie securely but loosely so pasta expands. Repeat for all rolls.
Cook and serve:
1. Heat tomato sauce smooth on side.
2. Poach rolls in hot chicken broth until cooked through (about 15 minutes). Simmer gently to prevent cheese from pushing out or pasta breaking.
3. Remove from broth, unwrap, cut into rounds about 2 inches thick.
4. Arrange slices with cut side up, pour sauce over plate, swirl lightly. Avoid covering pasta and cheese; use sauce as a background for visual effect.
5. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese and chopped parsley on top.
This recipe is versatile; try using Tony's Manicotti filling with four cheeses instead of the bland mixture.
